questions.question: For a visual search product where queries are text and gallery items are images, which backbone do you pick first?
options: A CLIP or SigLIP model — they learn a shared text-image embedding space so cosine similarity between text query and image gallery is meaningful out of the box
questions.explanation: Retrieval with text queries requires embeddings where text and image end up in the same space. CLIP/SigLIP train exactly this with the contrastive loss in Lesson 18. Supervised ImageNet features are image-only. DINOv2 is strong but image-only. Text queries demand a vision-language encoder.
questions.question_zh: 对于一个用户用文本查询、图库是图片的视觉搜索产品，第一个 backbone 应该选什么？
options_zh: CLIP 或 SigLIP 模型——它们学到了共享的文本-图像 embedding 空间，文本查询和图像图库之间的余弦相似度开箱即用
手工设计的 ORB 描述子
questions.explanation_zh: 用文本查询做检索要求 embedding 空间里文本和图像在同一个空间。CLIP/SigLIP 用第 18 课讲过的对比 loss 训练就是为了这个。监督式 ImageNet 特征只懂图像。DINOv2 很强但也只懂图像。文本查询必须用视觉-语言 encoder。

questions.question: What does 'semi-hard mining' mean in triplet-loss training?
options: For each anchor, select a negative that is further than the positive but still within the margin; easy negatives contribute no gradient and hardest negatives can destabilise training, so semi-hard is the sweet spot
Using only the hardest possible negatives
questions.explanation: Triplet loss gradient is non-zero only when d(a, n) < d(a, p) + margin. Easy negatives (already far) give zero gradient. Hardest negatives can collapse training. Semi-hard — d(a, p) < d(a, n) < d(a, p) + margin — is where the loss is informative without being unstable. FaceNet introduced this recipe in 2015 and it is still the default for triplet fine-tuning.
questions.question_zh: 在 triplet loss 训练里 'semi-hard mining' 是什么意思？
options_zh: 对每个 anchor，挑一个比正例更远但仍在 margin 范围内的负例；太简单的负例不产生梯度，最难的负例又会让训练不稳定，semi-hard 是甜蜜点
用挖矿来产生训练样本
questions.explanation_zh: Triplet loss 的梯度只在 d(a, n) < d(a, p) + margin 时非零。容易的负例（已经足够远）梯度为零。最难的负例又会让训练崩。Semi-hard——d(a, p) < d(a, n) < d(a, p) + margin——是 loss 既有信息量又稳定的区间。FaceNet 在 2015 年提出这个套路，至今仍是 triplet fine-tune 的默认做法。

questions.question: Your retrieval system reports recall@10 = 0.95 but recall@1 = 0.42. What should you conclude?
options: The embedding space has the correct structure — relevant items are usually in the top 10 — but ranking within the top 10 is noisy; a re-ranking model (cross-encoder, second-stage scorer) or longer metric-learning fine-tune will fix recall@1 without damaging recall@10
Data is leaking from gallery to queries
questions.explanation: recall@K vs @1 tells you where the information sits. A high recall@10 with low recall@1 means retrieval is approximately right but the model is not confident about ordering the top few. Re-ranking — a separate model that scores every top-K candidate against the query — is the production fix and is what search engines like Pinterest and Google Photos use.
questions.question_zh: 你的检索系统报告 recall@10 = 0.95、recall@1 = 0.42，应该得出什么结论？
options_zh: embedding 空间结构是对的——相关项基本都在 top 10 里——但 top 10 内部的排序很噪。加一个重排（cross-encoder、二阶段打分）或继续做 metric learning fine-tune 就能修好 recall@1，且不损害 recall@10
数据从 gallery 泄漏到了 query
questions.explanation_zh: recall@K 和 @1 的对比告诉你信息在哪。recall@10 高而 recall@1 低，说明检索大致正确，但模型对 top 几名的排序没有信心。重排——一个独立模型给每个 top-K 候选和 query 打分——是生产环境的常规修法，Pinterest、Google Photos 这类搜索都在用。

questions.question: Cosine similarity on unnormalised embeddings is not cosine similarity — what goes wrong?
options: It mixes direction with magnitude; cos(a, b) is only correct when both vectors are L2-normalised. On unnormalised embeddings, large-norm items dominate regardless of direction, skewing ranks toward vectors with big magnitudes
questions.explanation: cos(a, b) = (a . b) / (||a|| * ||b||). Skipping the normalisation gives you raw inner product, which ranks by direction weighted by magnitude. On typical neural embeddings magnitudes vary 2-3x across samples, and that alone dominates the ranking. Always L2-normalise before computing cosine or before indexing in FAISS IndexFlatIP.
questions.question_zh: 在没有归一化的 embedding 上算余弦相似度，其实算的根本不是余弦相似度——会出什么问题？
options_zh: 它把方向和模长混在一起；cos(a, b) 只有在两个向量都做了 L2 归一化时才正确。在没归一化的 embedding 上，模长大的样本不管方向如何都会主导排名
questions.explanation_zh: cos(a, b) = (a . b) / (||a|| * ||b||)。跳过归一化等于直接做内积，排序变成方向加权了模长。神经 embedding 的模长在样本之间通常差 2-3 倍，光这一项就能主导结果。算余弦或在 FAISS IndexFlatIP 里建索引前必须做 L2 归一化。

questions.question: Between instance-level retrieval (find this exact car) and category-level retrieval (find cars), which requires metric-learning fine-tuning?
options: Instance-level. Off-the-shelf DINOv2 and CLIP embeddings discriminate between categories well but not between visually similar instances of the same category. A triplet or contrastive fine-tune on (same instance, different instance) pairs tightens the embedding around each instance
Both require the same amount of fine-tuning
questions.explanation: Category-level retrieval is what pretrained models already do: cats cluster near cats, dogs near dogs. Instance-level — same car, same face, same product SKU — needs metric learning because two instances of the same product look very similar in the general embedding space. Fine-tuning with triplet or InfoNCE on instance-labelled pairs shrinks intra-instance distance and grows inter-instance distance.
questions.question_zh: instance 级检索（找这辆具体的车）和 category 级检索（找车）哪个需要 metric learning fine-tune？
options_zh: instance 级。开箱即用的 DINOv2 和 CLIP 在 category 之间区分良好，但在同一 category 内视觉相似的不同 instance 之间分不清。在 (同 instance, 不同 instance) 对上做 triplet 或对比 fine-tune，能把每个 instance 的 embedding 收紧
两者需要的 fine-tune 量一样
questions.explanation_zh: category 级检索是预训练模型本来就擅长的：猫聚在猫附近，狗聚在狗附近。Instance 级——同一辆车、同一张脸、同一个产品 SKU——必须做 metric learning，因为同一产品的两个 instance 在通用 embedding 空间里看起来非常像。在 instance 标注的样本对上用 triplet 或 InfoNCE fine-tune，能缩小 intra-instance 距离、放大 inter-instance 距离。
